title: Electron microscopy for virus identification and virus assemblage characterization
author: Kenneth M. Stedman
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.eahbab6
materials: 0.2 µm filtered and centrifuged cell-free supernatant

steps.title: Spot sample onto grid
steps.instruction: 5µL of an enrichment culture, or 0.2 µm filtered and centrifuged (10 min at 3000g) cell-free supernatant, is spotted onto carbon/formvar-coated electron microscope grids (Ted Pella or EM Sciences).

steps.title: Allow sample absorption
steps.instruction: It is then allowed to absorb for 2 min.

steps.title: Remove sample, stain, and air dry
steps.instruction: Remove sample from grid by slowly bringing a small (ca. 1cm 2 ) piece of filter paper perpendicular to the grid to the side of the grid. The sample will be removed by wicking. Stain grid by placing on a 5μL drop of 2% Uranyl Acetate (or Phosphotungstate) for 15-30 seconds. Remove stain by wicking as above. Air dry for at least 10 minutes.

steps.title: Examine samples by TEM
steps.instruction: Samples are examined by transmission electron microscopy (TEM), e.g., JEOL 100 cx, operated at 100 keV. VLPs can generally be discerned at ×16,000–20,000 magnification.
